commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Ethiopians. That is, as black as they, by your iniquities. (Challoner) Chus was father of the Scythians, Arabs Yet none of these nations were under the peculiar protection of God. The Israelites depended too much on this prerogative, (Calmet) which they deserved to lose by their sins. (Haydock)
God brought them out of Egypt. But he also took the Philistines from Caphtor, (Calmet) and enabled them to settle in the country. (Haydock)
Cappadocia. Cyprus, (Genesis x. 14.) or rather Crete, 1 Kings. (Calmet)
Cyrene, (Symmachus) "wall "(Theodotion) or "pit. "(Septuagint) Theglathphalassar took Aram or the people of Damascus into captivity. (Calmet)
Their future return is represented as already past. (Vatable; Mercer.)

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: As the children of the Ethiopians: That is, as black as they, by your iniquities.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
